methods.description: Describes the fossil material, anatomical conventions, measurement procedures, specimen housing, and comparison with other specimens and published data.

steps.description: The material consists of two passerine bird remains imprinted on three claystone slabs including an isolated sternum on one slab, and a nearly complete specimen on two slabs. Both specimens come from the same locality (Lavanttal, southern Austria).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and describe fossil material and locality.
inputs: Fossil specimens (two passerine bird remains on three claystone slabs)
outputs: Description of fossil material and locality

steps.description: Anatomical terminology follows Baumel and Witmer (1993), if not indicated otherwise.
Measurements, taken with Lux-Tool calipers on the original slabs, are given in millimeters and refer to the greatest length of the bone.
The fossils are housed at the paleontological collection of the Landesmuseum Kärnten (LMK-Pal), Klagenfurt, Austria.

They were compared with comparative specimens from the osteological collections of the Natural History Museum of Vienna, Austria, and the Institute of Systematics and Evolution of Animals, PAS, Kraków, Poland...
steps.searchable_summary: Compare fossil specimens to comparative osteological collections.
outputs: Observations on similarities/differences compared to collections

steps.description: ...and with published data on the osteology of extant and extinct Acanthisittidae, Oscines and Suboscines.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare fossil specimens to published osteological data.
outputs: Observations on similarities/differences compared to published data

methods.description: Procedure for partially exposing fossil skeletal elements from the surrounding matrix using mechanical tools.

steps.description: The nearly complete specimen was partially exposed mechanically by scraping off matrix covering the skeletal elements.
inputs: Nearly complete fossil specimen in matrix

steps.description: ...by scraping off matrix covering the skeletal elements.
steps.searchable_summary: Scrape off matrix covering skeletal elements.

steps.description: However, due to the fragility of the preserved remains and the overlapping of individual bones and their imprints, the fossil could not be fully extracted from the matrix.
steps.searchable_summary: Note limitations and fragility during preparation.

methods.description: Non-invasive imaging procedure using micro-computed tomography to visualize internal and external structures of the fossil specimens.

steps.description: The surface of both slabs was fixed with liquid glue...
steps.searchable_summary: Fix the surface of the slabs with liquid glue.

steps.description: ...the specimen was investigated non-invasively by micro-CT scanning at the Department of Palaeontology, University of Vienna, Austria using a SkyScan/Bruker 1173 micro-CT device with a brass filter. The main slab was scanned with source voltage at 100 kV, source current at 80 µA, image pixel size 29.93 µm, exposure time 750 ms, ring artifact correction 20, and beam hardening correction of 56%.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform micro-CT scan of main slab with specified parameters.
inputs: Scanning parameters (100 kV, 80 µA, 29.93 µm, 750 ms, ring artifact 20, beam hardening 56%)
outputs: CT scan data (sequence of images) for main slab

steps.description: The counter slab was scanned using source voltage at 100 kV, source current at 80 µA, image pixel size 26.01 µm, exposure time 750 ms, ring artifact correction 14, and beam hardening correction of 30%.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform micro-CT scan of counter slab with specified parameters.
inputs: Scanning parameters (100 kV, 80 µA, 26.01 µm, 750 ms, ring artifact 14, beam hardening 30%)
outputs: CT scan data (sequence of images) for counter slab

steps.description: Enough contrast between the fossil and the matrix was available to separate and identify fossil bone from the matrix.
steps.searchable_summary: Verify sufficient contrast between fossil and matrix in scan data.
inputs: CT scan data from main and counter slabs
outputs: Confirmation of adequate contrast for segmentation

methods.description: Procedure for processing micro-CT scan data, including importing, segmentation, and creating 3D models of specific skeletal elements.

steps.description: Scan data were imported in Amira 5.4.5 (FEI Visualization Sciences Group) as a sequence of stacked JPGs.
steps.searchable_summary: Import CT scan data (JPG sequence) into Amira software.
inputs: CT scan data (sequence of stacked JPGs)

steps.description: Segmentation was carried out using the lasso and brush tools.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform segmentation using lasso and brush tools in Amira.
outputs: Segmented regions (fossil bone separated from matrix)

steps.description: 3D reconstruction in Amira 5.4.5 was carried out for the right and left coracoids as well as right carpometacarpus and os carpi ulnare on the main slab...
steps.searchable_summary: Create 3D reconstruction of specified bones from the main slab segmentation in Amira.
inputs: Segmented data for main slab bones (right coracoid, left coracoid, right carpometacarpus, os carpi ulnare)
outputs: 3D models of specified bones from main slab

steps.description: ...and right carpometacarpus on the counter slab.
steps.searchable_summary: Create 3D reconstruction of specified bones from the counter slab segmentation in Amira.
inputs: Segmented data for counter slab bones (right carpometacarpus)
outputs: 3D models of specified bones from counter slab
